disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden chest tightness, breathlessness and a racing/skipping heartbeat came on this evening during and after an intense period of reading health information online
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when this started?
When exactly did the symptoms begin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started tonight while I was reading medical articles about a blood result of mine — my chest went tight, I couldn't get a full breath, and my heart started racing and skipping.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The chest discomfort does not radiate to the arm or jaw
trigger_question_examples: Does the tightness spread anywhere — into your arm, neck or jaw?
Is the pain only in your chest, or does it move?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's just in my chest — it doesn't spread into my arm or my jaw.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fainting or loss of consciousness, and no coughing up of blood
trigger_question_examples: Have you fainted or come close to passing out?
Have you coughed up any blood?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't fainted or passed out, and I'm definitely not coughing up any blood.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No recent long-haul travel, immobilisation, leg swelling or calf pain
trigger_question_examples: Any recent long flights, surgery or long periods off your feet?
Any swelling or pain in one of your calves?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No recent flights or surgery, I've been up and about as usual, and my legs feel completely normal — no swelling or pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A strong, escalating sense of dread and fear of dying, closely tied to reading alarming health information
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling emotionally right now?
What are you most afraid is happening?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel this overwhelming dread, like I'm about to die — it got much worse the more I read about my condition online tonight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A background of a mild low-titer ANA finding, intermittent cold-triggered colour change in the fingers, and mild fatigue, with no current medications
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any ongoing medical conditions or recent test results?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: A blood test showed a mild 'ANA' result that I was told probably means nothing. My fingers go pale in the cold sometimes and I get a bit more tired than before. I'm not on any medications.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Prior milder episodes of chest tightness and palpitations occurring in the context of health-related anxiety
trigger_question_examples: Have you had anything like this before?
Do these feelings come on at particular times?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've had milder versions before when I've been really worried about my health, but never as bad as tonight.
